history.content:
<thought>
I am near the top-left area facing right. The cell directly ahead is lava, so I should not move forward. The safest immediate progress is to turn downward and then navigate around the lava column toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am now facing down in the same position. Moving forward would step into lava below, so I need to avoid that. Turning left will face me toward the open path to the left, which lets me route around the obstacle safely.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am back facing right, with lava directly ahead. To make progress safely, I need to head downward toward the open corridor, so I should turn right to face down.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
Facing down would put me into lava if I move forward, so I should reorient to the left or right to navigate around it. Turning left will face me toward the open space on the left side.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing right again, and there is lava directly in front of me, so moving forward is unsafe. Turning right will face me downward, which lets me start moving around the obstacle.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing down, but the cell ahead is lava. To avoid it, I need to turn so I can move along the open corridor instead of entering lava. Turning right will face me left, away from the obstacle.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am now facing left, and the cell ahead is a wall, so I cannot move forward. Turning left will face me down, which is the next step toward navigating around the blockage.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
Facing down again, but the cell directly ahead is lava, so moving forward is unsafe. I need to change direction to continue through the open area; turning right will face me left, which avoids the lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing left at the top-left open cell, but the cell ahead is a wall, so I can’t advance. To get around the obstacle and toward the open corridor below, I should turn down.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m back facing down at the top-left open cell. The cell ahead is lava, so I should not move forward. To head into the open path, I need to face right, which requires a counter-clockwise turn from down.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing right, and the cell ahead is lava, so moving forward is unsafe. To avoid it, I should turn right to face down and then continue along the open area.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am back facing down at the top-left open cell, and the cell ahead is lava. I need to navigate around the obstacle, so I should turn right to face left? No, that would point into the wall. The open path is to my right, so I need to face right, which from down requires a counter-clockwise turn.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing right again, but lava is directly ahead. To get around it, I should turn downward so I can move into the open corridor below.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at the top-left area facing down. The path below is open, so I should move forward to continue around the lava barrier.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am still facing down, and the cell directly ahead is clear. Moving forward keeps me progressing through the open corridor toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing down and the cell ahead is lava, so moving forward would be unsafe. I should turn right to face the open path toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing left, and the cell ahead is a wall. To continue toward the open area and the goal, I should turn down toward the safer path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing down, but the cell ahead is blocked by lava. I should turn right to face the open corridor and keep progressing toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am back facing left, which means I likely turned into a dead end or unsafe direction. The open path toward the goal is to the right/down through the corridor, so I should rotate to face down next.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing down again, and the cell directly ahead is lava, so moving forward would be unsafe. I need to turn right to face the open path toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing left with a lava cell directly ahead, so I should not move forward. The open route toward the goal is to the right, so I need to rotate right to face down? Wait, from left-facing, turning right makes me face up; turning left makes me face down. Since the corridor below is blocked by lava too, I should choose the safer orientation toward the open area by turning right to re-evaluate the path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing up, and the cell ahead is open, while the goal is lower on the map. I need to turn right to face the open corridor toward the goal area.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing right, and the path ahead is open. Moving forward will advance me along the corridor toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
